content_of_post_to_comment: I've been chronically online for the better part of 20 years, yet I feel like i haven't really learned anything that meaningful from the internet over those 20 years. Once I turned off my computer and started spending my time offline, my life has changed. It is never too late to start.
comment: I had a similar realization that being constantly connected can feel like learning, but a lot of it is just collecting fragments and opinions. The internet is great for finding information, but it rarely gives you the time to sit with an idea long enough to turn it into wisdom. Getting offline creates that space. I'm curious what parts of life changed the most after you stepped away?
